intended_use: Overjet Caries Assist (OCA) is a radiological, automated, concurrent-read, computer-assisted detection (CADe) software intended to aid in the detection and segmentation of caries on bitewing and peria
intended_use_full: Overjet Caries Assist (OCA) is a radiological, automated, concurrent-read, computer-assisted detection (CADe) software intended to aid in the detection and segmentation of caries on bitewing and periapical radiographs. The device provides additional information for the dentist to use in their diagnosis of a tooth surface suspected of being carious. The device is not intended as a replacement for a complete dentist's review or their clinical judgment that takes into account other relevant information from the image, patient history, or actual in vivo clinical assessment.
ground_truth: Consensus labels of three US licensed dentists with adjudication by an oral radiologist for non-consensus labels
other_metrics: Dice coefficient for lesion segmentation: bitewing primary caries 0.77, secondary caries 0.73, enamel 0.76, dentin 0.77; periapical primary caries 0.79, secondary caries 0.79, enamel 0.75, dentin 0.81. Reader study sensitivity improvement: bitewing 64.6% to 78.5%, periapical 65.6% to 79.0%. Reader study specificity: bitewing 99.0% to 98.6%, periapical 98.0% to 97.6%.

safety_events.narrative_text: THE 510(K) SUBSTANTIAL EQUIVALENCE REPORT FOR OVERJET CARIES ASSIST (K212519) SHOWS SENSITIVITY AND SPECIFICITY OF 72.0% AND 98.1%, RESPECTIVELY, FOR CARIES DETECTION. HOWEVER, CALCULATIONS BASED ON THE TABLE IN SECTION 10 REVEAL A POSITIVE PREDICTIVE VALUE OF ONLY 57.2%. THIS MEANS THAT 42.8% OF OBSERVED CARIES BY THE OVERJET PRODUCT WERE NOT ACTUALLY CARIES ACCORDING TO GROUND TRUTH. SUCH A HIGH RATE OF FALSE POSITIVES HAS THE POTENTIAL TO RESULT IN OVER-TREATMENT DUE TO FALSELY IDENTIFYING A CARIOUS LESION WHEN NONE EXISTS. THE POSITIVE AND NEGATIVE PREDICTIVE VALUES WERE NOT REPORTED IN THE PERFORMANCE TESTING AND DO NOT APPEAR TO HAVE BEEN CONSIDERED BY THE 510(K) PROCESS.
